message:
feat(runtime): wire CONFIG GET/SET for client-output-buffer-limit, fix proto-max-bulk-len parser enforcement, and correct Redis default

CONFIG GET now reports client-query-buffer-limit, proto-max-bulk-len, and
client-output-buffer-limit. CONFIG SET for client-output-buffer-limit validates
non-negative integer and updates the live runtime state. Two new tests verify
proto-max-bulk-len rejects oversized bulk strings at the parser level, and
client-output-buffer-limit round-trips through CONFIG SET/GET.

parser_config() now takes the minimum of the policy gate's max_bulk_len and
the runtime's proto_max_bulk_len, ensuring CONFIG SET proto-max-bulk-len
actually constrains the wire parser rather than only updating a stored value.

Corrects proto_max_bulk_len default from 512 MiB (536870912) to Redis's
actual default of 512000000 (512 MB in decimal, not binary). Also replaces a
duplicated ParserConfig construction in execute_bytes with the shared
parser_config() method and applies rustfmt to .map_err closures.

Marks the now-unused crc16() in fr-store with #[allow(dead_code)] since the
DUMP/RESTORE upgrade to CRC64 made it unreferenced.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
